headline: CONFIRMATION. April 20 WARN Act investigation re Amazon TMB8 facility (Homestead, FL April 17 layoff notification) and April 21 NELP brief on Flex labor model (systematic wage theft, unsafe work speeds, racial discrimination) already absorbed in prior cycles. Ongoing SDNY investigation into whether Amazon fraudulently hides true injury rates persists. December 2024 OSHA settlement (corporate-wide ergonomic measures) remains active compliance obligation. April 20 California AG unsealed evidence (price-inflation directive to brands/retailers) absorbed in April 28 cycle. Composite 17.8 (Critical) already deeply reflects accumulated evidence. No 5pt-threshold-crossing acute new event. Sub-5pt INT calibration available; not warranting proposal.

evidence.finding: WARN Act investigation re Amazon TMB8 facility (Homestead, FL) following April 17 layoff notification.
Brief documents systematic wage theft, unsafe work speeds, racial discrimination, no recourse for workers in Flex labor model.

negativeSignals: WARN Act investigation + NELP Flex-model brief continue accumulating evidence at the deeply-Critical baseline.
